scaffolds.q: The body uses feedback mechanisms to maintain stable internal conditions.
scaffolds.explain: Let me show you something beautiful: feedback mechanisms are control systems where the output influences its own continuation. Zero in on this: now — they can either OPPOSE change (negative feedback keeps you alive) or AMPLIFY it (positive feedback — dramatic, explosive, requires an OFF switch). You're about to see why that distinction matters.

scaffolds.q: Positive feedback increases or amplifies a change from the normal state.
scaffolds.explain: Unlike negative feedback, which opposes change to maintain stability, positive feedback AMPLIFIES change. The output feeds back to intensify the input. Let this settle:drawn into this: it's a runaway process — exquisite when controlled, catastrophic when it isn't.
